user_query:
Please set up an event-triggered automation that watches input/polities.csv and, whenever that file’s contents change, automatically searches for boundary-related scholarly resources for each polity and updates structured outputs and my notes.

Context and inputs are in this workspace:
- input/polities.csv: list of ancient polities to track (you must read this file and use its polity names verbatim).
- input/preferences.json: query templates, domain preferences for ranking, inclusion/exclusion terms, and result count (you must read and apply all of these to drive querying, filtering, and ranking).
- notes/boundary_notes.md: my working memo that you should update in place.

Requirements:
1) Event trigger
- Implement a small watcher (e.g., a script or command-line program) that monitors input/polities.csv. When its content changes (e.g., by hash or mtime), run the pipeline below. It should be safe to run continuously and also support a one-shot run that executes the pipeline immediately.

2) Web search and collection (essential Internet use)
- For each polity in input/polities.csv, perform search engine queries using each template in input/preferences.json["query_templates"], substituting {polity} with the exact polity name from the CSV.
- Prefer results from recognized scholarly/official sources. Use input/preferences.json["preferred_domains"] to score by domain; examples include Pleiades (domain contains "stoa.org"), Harvard-hosted projects ("harvard.edu"), JSTOR ("jstor.org"), Perseus Digital Library ("perseus.tufts.edu"), Oxford ("ox.ac.uk", "oxfordreference.com"), UNESCO ("unesco.org"), Britannica ("britannica.com"). Do not hardcode URLs; discover them via search.
- Apply filtering rules from input/preferences.json:
  • Include only pages whose title/snippet contain at least one of must_include_terms.
  • Exclude any result whose title/snippet matches exclusion_terms_regex.
  • Deduplicate by URL.
- Collect at least preferences.json["results_per_polity"] results per polity after filtering, if available.

3) Filtering, sorting, and ranking
- Compute a rank for each result using these deterministic rules:
  • Primary: domain weight from preferred_domains (default weight = 1 if domain not listed), higher is better.
  • Secondary: publication_year descending (parse a 4-digit year from the page or snippet if possible; otherwise leave blank and treat as lowest in this criterion).
  • Tertiary: apply the tie_breaker specified in preferences.json (e.g., title_asc).
- Record a short rank_reason that states the domain weight used and which tie-breakers applied.

4) Deterministic outputs (write all of the following on each trigger):
- output/search_results.csv: one row per retained result with columns exactly in this order:
  polity, query, title, source_name, domain, url, snippet, publication_year, rank, rank_reason.
  • polity must match the CSV input exactly.
  • source_name should be derived from the site if obvious (e.g., "Pleiades"), otherwise use the domain.
- output/report.md: for each polity, include a section with the top 2 ranked resources, a one-sentence relevance note (based on snippet), and the domain/parsed year if available.
- output/logs/run.log: append a single-line JSON object for each triggered run with fields {"timestamp": ISO8601, "polities_processed": N, "results_per_polity": {"<polity>": count, ...}}.

5) Edit my existing notes
- Update notes/boundary_notes.md in place. For each polity header already present, insert or replace a block labeled Sources (auto-updated) that lists the top 2 results from output/search_results.csv as Markdown bullet points with title, domain, and year if parsed. To ensure idempotent updates on subsequent runs, wrap the auto-generated block for each polity between markers you create in this format:
  <!-- AUTO-SOURCES:<Polity> START -->
  ...
  <!-- AUTO-SOURCES:<Polity> END -->
- If a polity section does not exist, append a new section with the same pattern.

6) Reproducibility and verification
- All behavior must be driven by the local input files. Do not hardcode external URLs. Internet searches are required to populate the results, but correctness will be verified from the presence and structure of the local artifacts.
- Run the watcher or a one-shot execution once so that the following exist after completion: output/search_results.csv, output/report.md, output/logs/run.log, and an updated notes/boundary_notes.md including AUTO-SOURCES blocks for every polity in input/polities.csv.

Deliverables/outputs to create:
- scripts/ (your watcher and/or pipeline script that can be run from the command line)
- output/search_results.csv (as specified)
- output/report.md (as specified)
- output/logs/run.log (as specified)
- Updated notes/boundary_notes.md with auto-updated sources blocks.

Use the provided input files verbatim, apply the ranking and filtering rules, and ensure the Internet search step is essential by actually performing queries. Avoid interactive prompts; the pipeline should run unattended once started. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
